title: A stranger handed me an envelope at my rehearsal dinner proving my fiance was still married
script: Ten minutes before our rehearsal dinner toast, a woman I had never met walked up holding a manila envelope and said, "You should know what he really did." She said her name was Bianca, his college girlfriend he swore they never made official. Inside the envelope was a marriage certificate from 2019, his signature right next to hers, still legally valid, never annulled. My hands were shaking when I found him by the bar and asked if it was real. He did not deny it. He said he meant to file the annulment for six years and just kept putting it off. Six years of anniversaries, six years of "I love you," six years of promises made to me while he was still legally someone else's husband. I asked him one question: did he know this before he proposed to me. He looked at the floor instead of answering, and that silence told me more than the certificate ever could.
